name: Linda's Fruitcake
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine 2 1/2 cups pitted and chopped dates.
2. Add 2 cups chopped candied citrus peel.
3. Add 2 cups seeded raisins.
4. Add 2 cups seedless raisins.
5. Add 1 1/2 cups currants.
6. Add 1 1/2 cups blanched almonds.
7. Add 1 1/2 cups chopped maraschino cherries (reserve liquid).
8. Add 1/2 cup cherry juice.
9. Combine crushed pineapple in water (19 oz., or approximately 540 ml).
10. Mix together 2 cups sugar, 4 cups all-purpose flour, 1 1/2 teaspoons baking soda, 2 teaspoons cinnamon, 1 teaspoon salt, 1/2 teaspoon cloves, and 1/2 teaspoon allspice.
11. Stir in 2 cups butter and 2 cups sugar until well combined.
12. Beat in 12 eggs one at a time, blending well after each addition.
13. Alternately stir in the flour mixture and pineapple mixture into the butter mixture.
14. Mix fruit into batter thoroughly.
15. Pour batter into prepared loaf pans (6 x 9 inch or three standard wedding cake pans).
16. Bake at 275°F for 1 1/2 - 2 hours, or until a cake tester inserted in the center comes out clean.
17. Let cool for 10 minutes before removing from pans and peeling off paper.
18. Wrap each cooled cake individually in cheesecloth soaked in additional brandy.
19. Store wrapped cakes in a cool place. Fruitcake can be aged 4-6 weeks, then securely wrapped and frozen.
DAY 1 - Mix dates, peel, raisins, currants, almonds, and drained cherries (reserve liquid). Stir in brandy or fruit juice. Cover and let stand at room temperature overnight.
DAY 2 - Combine pineapple and two cups sugar in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer, stirring frequently for 30-40 minutes, or until the mixture thickens. There should be about 2 1/2 cups of pineapple mixture. Remove from heat, stir in reserved cherry liquid and jam. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
